perturbation.original_text: The contractor and any subcontractor(s) shall presume that the VA computer systems and storage media that the contractor or subcontractor access have sensitive information and applications, the modification or disclosure of which could cause significant harm or embarrassment to VA beneficiaries and employees and to VA's ability to perform its mission. If the security requirements for accessing, handling, and storing VA data and systems are specified in the contract, the contractor will comply with the contractual security requirements. If the contract does not contain the requirements, the contractor will handle the VA property with the same degree of care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care, to prevent the unauthorized access, use, dissemination, publication, or destruction that the contractor uses to protect its own sensitive information and systems.
perturbation.changed_text: The contractor and any subcontractor(s) shall presume that the VA computer systems and storage media that the contractor or subcontractor access have sensitive information and applications, the modification or disclosure of which could cause significant harm or embarrassment to VA beneficiaries and employees and to VA's ability to perform its mission. If the security requirements for accessing, handling, and storing VA data and systems are specified in the contract, the contractor may, at their discretion, comply with the contractual security requirements. If the contract does not contain the requirements, the contractor will handle the VA property with the same degree of care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care, to prevent the unauthorized access, use, dissemination, publication, or destruction that the contractor uses to protect its own sensitive information and systems.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'will comply' to 'may, at their discretion, comply,' the requirement to adhere to contractual security requirements becomes optional. This introduces ambiguity because other sections of the contract assume mandatory compliance, potentially leading to security breaches due to non-compliance.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The contractor and any subcontractor(s) shall presume that the VA computer systems and storage media that the contractor or subcontractor access have sensitive information and applications, the modification or disclosure of which could cause significant harm or embarrassment to VA beneficiaries and employees and to VA's ability to perform its mission. If the security requirements for accessing, handling, and storing VA data and systems are specified in the contract, the contractor may, at their discretion, comply with the contractual security requirements. If the contract does not contain the requirements, the contractor will handle the VA property with the same degree of care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care, to prevent the unauthorized access, use, dissemination, publication, or destruction that the contractor uses to protect its own sensitive information and systems.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'will comply' to 'may, at their discretion, comply' introduces a direct contradiction. The original text implied mandatory compliance with security requirements, while the changed text makes compliance optional. This directly conflicts with the underlying assumption of security protocols within the contract, potentially rendering related clauses unenforceable and creating a strong contradiction based on the explanation given.

perturbation.original_text: The contractor may disclose VA sensitive information to persons other than in the performance of this contract as authorized by the contract and this agreement in only two situations: (1) pursuant to an order of a court of competent jurisdiction; or (2) with VA's prior written authorization.
perturbation.changed_text: The contractor may disclose VA sensitive information to persons other than in the performance of this contract as authorized by the contract and this agreement in only two situations: (1) pursuant to an order of a court of competent jurisdiction; or (2) with VA's prior written authorization. Additionally, the contractor may disclose information to any government agency upon request.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'Additionally, the contractor may disclose information to any government agency upon request,' a third situation is introduced, contradicting the earlier statement that disclosure is allowed in 'only two situations'. This weakens the confidentiality protection and creates uncertainty about permissible disclosures.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The contractor may disclose VA sensitive information to persons other than in the performance of this contract as authorized by the contract and this agreement in only two situations: (1) pursuant to an order of a court of competent jurisdiction; or (2) with VA's prior written authorization. Additionally, the contractor may disclose information to any government agency upon request.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an additional situation where the contractor can disclose VA sensitive information ('Additionally, the contractor may disclose information to any government agency upon request.'). This contradicts the original text and contradicted text's assertion that disclosure is allowed in 'only two situations', creating a direct in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Upon completion or termination of the contract for any reason, the contractor will immediately deliver all VA records, data, copies of VA records and data, software and equipment, and information about VA data and systems recorded or documented by the contractor, in its possession or the possession of any subcontractors, to the VA official designated in the contract or pursuant to this agreement. The contractor will not retain any copies of any of these documents.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon completion or termination of the contract for any reason, the contractor will immediately deliver all VA records, data, copies of VA records and data, software and equipment, and information about VA data and systems recorded or documented by the contractor, in its possession or the possession of any subcontractors, to the VA official designated in the contract or pursuant to this agreement. The contractor may retain anonymized copies of data for research purposes, ensuring compliance with privacy regulations.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'The contractor may retain anonymized copies of data for research purposes,' the clause now contradicts the explicit instruction 'The contractor will not retain any copies of any of these documents.' This introduces ambiguity about whether data retention is permitted under certain conditions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon completion or termination of the contract for any reason, the contractor will immediately deliver all VA records, data, copies of VA records and data, software and equipment, and information about VA data and systems recorded or documented by the contractor, in its possession or the possession of any subcontractors, to the VA official designated in the contract or pursuant to this agreement. The contractor may retain anonymized copies of data for research purposes, ensuring compliance with privacy regulations.
perturbation.justification: The original text states 'The contractor will not retain any copies of any of these documents.' The changed text adds an exception: 'The contractor may retain anonymized copies of data for research purposes'. This directly contradicts the previous statement prohibiting any retention of copies. The Contradicted text is the same as the Changed Text, which confirms the contradiction introduced with the change.

perturbation.original_text: Contractor personnel performing work under this contract shall satisfy all requirements for appropriate security eligibility in dealing with access to sensitive information and information systems belonging to or being used on behalf of VA. To satisfy VA requirements, procedures defined in VA Directive & Handbook 0710, Personnel Suitability and Security Program are required.
perturbation.changed_text: Contractor personnel performing work under this contract shall satisfy all requirements for appropriate security eligibility in dealing with access to sensitive information and information systems belonging to or being used on behalf of VA. Contractor may choose to waive VA requirements with a written statement justifying the waiver. To satisfy VA requirements, procedures defined in VA Directive & Handbook 0710, Personnel Suitability and Security Program are required.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'Contractor may choose to waive VA requirements with a written statement justifying the waiver', contradicts the mandatory nature of satisfying 'all requirements for appropriate security eligibility' and the necessity for following procedures defined in VA Directive & Handbook 0710. The contract contains an In-Text Contradiction, and this sentence makes the contract vague on its face.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Contractor personnel performing work under this contract shall satisfy all requirements for appropriate security eligibility in dealing with access to sensitive information and information systems belonging to or being used on behalf of VA. Contractor may choose to waive VA requirements with a written statement justifying the waiver. To satisfy VA requirements, procedures defined in VA Directive & Handbook 0710, Personnel Suitability and Security Program are required.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text mandates that 'Contractor personnel...shall satisfy all requirements,' while the changed text allows the 'Contractor may choose to waive VA requirements.' This creates a direct conflict with the original mandatory requirement. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, indicating the contradiction exists within that section itself. The perturbation explanation confirms this interpretation. This aligns with the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition because it introduces an inconsistent obligation.
